utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. Now we check the final coverage plan to see if each is present and fully specified. (1) health_coverage: present with provider, coverage types, included items, costs — fully specified. (2) property_coverage: present with provider, coverage details, limits, and costs — fully specified. (3) travel_coverage: present with provider, coverage_type, regions, duration, and costs — fully specified. (4) additional_services: present with sub-items, coverage details, and costs — fully specified. (5) total_budget: present with monthly and annual breakdown — fully specified. All 5 items appear and are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists four main insurance components and additional services. To map them to ground-truth ratings: (1) The 'health_coverage' provider is HealthFirst, which corresponds to 'HealthFirst Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage → physical_health with rating 8. (2) The 'mental_health_coverage' provider is also HealthFirst, corresponding to 'HealthFirst Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage → mental_health with rating 8. (3) The 'travel_coverage' provider is HealthFirst, but the ground truth only lists travel plans under mobility_coverage → travel_insurance. The closest match is 'TravelFirst Standard Plan' (similar naming convention to HealthFirst), rated 9. (4) The 'property_coverage' provider is LifeFirst, matching 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' under property_protection → home_content, rated 9. (5) Under 'additional_services', there is an 'adventure_addon' which aligns with the 'Adventure Travel Coverage' under travel_enhancement_services, rated 10. (6) The 'wellness_programs' add-on corresponds to 'Wellness Programs' under health_enhancement_services, rated 3.
